issue_title: Stop button never cancels the agent run: cancellationSignal run() parameter shadows the constructor field
issue_body: ### What happened? Tapping **Stop** during an active agent run sets `MainActivity.agentCancellationSignal = true` ([MainActivity.kt:1774](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/MainActivity.kt#L1774)), but the cancellation check inside the agent loop never observes it. The run continues to completion as if Stop was never pressed; `AgentEvent.RunCancelled` is never emitted and the final stop step lands with `COMPLETED` (or `MAX_STEPS`) instead of `USER_CANCELLED`. The root cause is a **parameter-shadows-field** mistake repeated at two layers of the cancellation pipeline. `BoundedLocalAgentLoop` ([BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt:15-31](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt#L15-L31)) and `AgentRunner` ([AgentRunner.kt:14-38](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L14-L38)) both declare a constructor field named `cancellationSignal` **and** a `run()` method parameter with the same name and a default of `AtomicBoolean(false)`: ```kotlin // BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt class BoundedLocalAgentLoop( ... private val cancellationSignal: AtomicBoolean = AtomicBoolean(false), // line 22 — field ... ) { fun run( ... cancellationSignal: AtomicBoolean = AtomicBoolean(false) // line 30 — parameter, shadows the field ): AgentRunResult { ... if (cancellationSignal.get()) { // line 55 — reads the PARAMETER, never the field ... return stopped(... AgentStepStopReason.USER_CANCELLED) } } } ``` `AgentRunner` has the same shape ([AgentRunner.kt:21](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L21) field, [AgentRunner.kt:29](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L29) parameter), and the field is then re-threaded into the inner overload at [AgentRunner.kt:50-66](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L50-L66) which constructs `BoundedLocalAgentLoop` and immediately also passes the **local parameter** to `BoundedLocalAgentLoop.run(...)`, defeating the constructor write. In production the chain that loses the signal is: 1. [MainActivity.kt:870](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/MainActivity.kt#L870) — `reasoningCore.run(... cancellationSignal = agentCancellationSignal)`. The real signal enters the system here. 2. [LocalReasoningCore.kt:164-170](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/LocalReasoningCore.kt#L164-L170) — `LocalReasoningCore.run` forwards it to the `AgentRunInvocation` lambda's `cancellationSignal` parameter. 3. [LocalReasoningCore.kt:271-286](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/LocalReasoningCore.kt#L271-L286) — `defaultAgentRunInvocation` calls `buildDefaultAgentRunner(... cancellationSignal = cancellationSignal).run(task, listener = listener, timeline = timeline)`. The **constructor** of `AgentRunner` is given the real signal — but the `.run(...)` call **does not** pass it. So `AgentRunner.run()`'s `cancellationSignal` parameter falls back to its default `AtomicBoolean(false)` and shadows the field. 4. [AgentRunner.kt:31-37](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L31-L37) — the no-default-override 4-arg `run` overload forwards the local (false) signal to the 5-arg overload. 5. [AgentRunner.kt:50-66](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/AgentRunner.kt#L50-L66) — the 5-arg overload constructs `BoundedLocalAgentLoop(cancellationSignal = cancellationSignal /* false */)` and then `.run(... cancellationSignal = cancellationSignal /* false */)`. 6. [BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt:55](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt#L55) — `if (cancellationSignal.get())` always sees `false`. The cancellation branch is unreachable. Net effect: the user's real signal is silently discarded twice — once by `AgentRunner.run()`'s parameter-shadowing default, and again by `BoundedLocalAgentLoop.run()`'s parameter-shadowing default. The Stop button looks active in the UI but does nothing observable in the agent loop. This regression was introduced in commit `91c77c6` (`feat: add running agent state controls`) together with `dce22eb` (`feat: add cancellationSignal to BoundedLocalAgentLoop`) and `0ac908a` (`fix: merge conflicts and add RunCancelled support`). None of those commits added an end-to-end cancellation test, so no existing test fails on the broken behavior. ### Expected behavior When `MainActivity.agentCancellationSignal` is set to `true` while a run is in progress, the next iteration of the loop in `BoundedLocalAgentLoop.run` MUST observe it at the cancellation check ([BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt:55](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt#L55)) and stop the run with `AgentStepStopReason.USER_CANCELLED`, emitting an `AgentEvent.RunCancelled` and a final stop step. Specifically: - A signal set BEFORE `run()` starts must stop the run at step 1 with `USER_CANCELLED`. - A signal set BETWEEN steps must stop the run at the next iteration boundary. - A run with no cancellation must finish normally with `COMPLETED` / `MAX_STEPS` / `REPEATED_TOOL_FAILURE`. - The `AgentRunner` and `BoundedLocalAgentLoop` constructor-supplied `cancellationSignal` must be the single source of truth — there is no reason for `run()` to accept a second `cancellationSignal` parameter that can disagree with the field. ### Steps to reproduce The bug is at the run-loop level, so the cleanest repro is a JVM unit test against `BoundedLocalAgentLoop` directly. There is no existing end-to-end cancellation test. 1. From the touchpilot project root, add a temporary regression test at `app/src/test/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/CancellationShadowingProbe.kt`: ```kotlin package dev.touchpilot.app.agent import dev.touchpilot.app.security.ToolApprovalProvider import dev.touchpilot.app.security.ToolSource import dev.touchpilot.app.tools.AndroidToolCatalog import dev.touchpilot.app.tools.ToolResult import dev.touchpilot.app.tools.ToolSpec import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean import kotlin.test.Test import kotlin.test.assertEquals import kotlin.test.assertTrue class CancellationShadowingProbe { @Test fun cancellationSignalSetBeforeRunStopsAtStepOne() { val signal = AtomicBoolean(false) val loop = BoundedLocalAgentLoop( tools = FakeTools(listOf(ToolResult(ok = true, message = "ok"))), approvalProvider = ToolApprovalProvider { true }, commandProvider = SequenceCommandProvider( listOf( """{"tool":"observe_screen","args":{}}""", """{"tool":"observe_screen","args":{}}""" ) ), source = ToolSource.LOCAL_ROUTER, cancellationSignal = signal ) // External "Stop" before any step runs. signal.set(true) // Caller (e.g. defaultAgentRunInvocation at LocalReasoningCore.kt:285) // does NOT pass the signal again to run(); it relies on the // constructor-supplied signal. val result = loop.run("observe twice", AgentRunLimits(maxSteps = 5)) assertEquals(AgentStepStopReason.USER_CANCELLED, result.stopReason) assertEquals(1, result.steps.size) assertTrue(result.events.any { it is AgentEvent.RunCancelled }) } private class SequenceCommandProvider(commands: List) : AgentCommandProvider { private val queue = ArrayDeque(commands) override fun complete(systemPrompt: String, context: String): String { return queue.removeFirstOrNull() ?: """{"final":"No more commands."}""" } } private class FakeTools(results: List) : LocalAgentLoopTools { private val q = ArrayDeque(results) override fun observeScreen(): String = "Home screen" override fun validate(name: String, args: Map) = AndroidToolCatalog.validate(name, args) override fun findTool(name: String): ToolSpec? = AndroidToolCatalog.find(name) override fun execute(name: String, args: Map, source: ToolSource) = q.removeFirst() } } ``` 2. Run only the new test class: ``` .\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest --tests dev.touchpilot.app.agent.CancellationShadowingProbe ``` 3. Observe one failure on `main` (commit `9884d46`): ``` CancellationShadowingProbe > cancellationSignalSetBeforeRunStopsAtStepOne FAILED org.junit.ComparisonFailure: expected: but was: 1 test completed, 1 failed ``` The run completed normally even though the cancellation signal was set to `true` before `run()` started — proof that `BoundedLocalAgentLoop.run()`'s local `cancellationSignal` parameter shadowed the constructor field. 4. **On LDPlayer** (already running, screenshots below from `D:\LDPlayer\LDPlayer9\dnplayer.exe` instance 0 at 720×1440): - Build + install the debug APK from `main` at `9884d46`, launch `dev.touchpilot.app/.MainActivity`. - In the **Chat** tab, type any task that will reach an action step before terminating — e.g. `open settings then go back`. The local router picks up the navigation phrase and queues a tool action that requires approval. - Send the message. The header row above the chat changes to **Running** with a red **Stop** button on the right; an `Approval requested - press_back` card appears below the agent-steps card while the loop sits waiting for approval. Capture: `bug6-before-stop.png`. ![before stop](bug6-before-stop.png) - Tap **Stop** on the running-state row. - Observed (`bug6-after-stop-chat.png`): the chat appends the UI-side message **"Run cancelled. Stopped by user request."** at the bottom, so it *looks* like Stop worked. But the underlying agent loop did **not** observe the cancellation: - The agent-steps card still shows `Act` as **PENDING** several seconds after Stop, not `STOPPED` with reason `USER_CANCELLED`. - The approval card flipped to `Rejected - press_back`, meaning the loop unblocked via the *approval-rejection* path — not via the cancellation check at [BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt:55](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/agent/BoundedLocalAgentLoop.kt#L55). - The "Working on it." bot bubble retains its accent border (UI considers the run still in flight). ![after stop — chat](bug6-after-stop-chat.png) - Tap the **Logs** tab. The tool-execution log line corroborates the path the loop actually took (`bug6-after-stop-logs.png`): ``` [16:27:18] press_back(risk=MEDIUM) -> fail: denied by user: medium risk Android action ``` The press_back attempt was *denied by user* — the approval-rejection path — rather than not running at all because of an observed cancellation signal. If `cancellationSignal.get()` at the top of the loop iteration had returned `true`, the step would never have reached approval, the tool would never have been queued for execution, and this log line would not exist. ![after stop — logs](bug6-after-stop-logs.png) The "Run cancelled" chat bubble that makes Stop *look* like it worked comes from the UI-side handler in `MainActivity.cancelAgentRun()` ([MainActivity.kt:1784](app/src/main/java/dev/touchpilot/app/MainActivity.kt#L1784)), which writes the bubble unconditionally whenever the user taps Stop. It is independent of whether the agent loop ever observes the signal. The loop's own `USER_CANCELLED` stop step is never emitted — exactly matching the JVM probe in step 3 above. Delete the regression test file once observed; this issue does not propose committing it. ### Device or emulator JVM (`./gradlew testDebugUnitTest`); no emulator required for the unit-test repro. The on-device repro was performed against LDPlayer 9 (`D:\LDPlayer\LDPlayer9\dnplayer.exe`), instance index 0, 720×1440 @ 240 dpi, on Windows 10 Pro x64. ### Android version Android 9 Pie, API level 28 (irrelevant for the JVM repro). ### TouchPilot commit `9884d464bc71abf388e9cd59ab0bb6e14631b00b` ### Accessibility service enabled? Yes (irrelevant for the JVM repro). ### Areas involved - [ ] Android control - [x] Agent loop - [ ] Local router / local inference - [ ] Skills - [ ] MCP - [ ] Security / approvals - [ ] UI ### Logs, screenshots, or traces The three captures referenced in the **Steps to reproduce** section above were taken on LDPlayer 9 against a debug APK built from `9884d46`: - `bug -before-stop.png` — Running state, red **Stop** button visible top-right; approval card pending below. - `bug-after-stop-chat.png` — several seconds after tapping Stop: UI says **"Run cancelled. Stopped by user request."** while the agent-steps card still has `Act: PENDING` and the approval card has flipped to `Rejected - press_back` (loop took the rejection path, not the cancellation path). - `bug-after-stop-logs.png` — Logs tab `ToolExecutionLog` line `press_back(risk=MEDIUM) -> fail: denied by user: medium risk Android action` proving the loop unblocked via approval rejection rather than observing the cancellation signal. JVM probe output (deterministic, no emulator required): ``` CancellationShadowingProbe > cancellationSignalSetBeforeRunStopsAtStepOne FAILED org.junit.ComparisonFailure: expected: but was: 1 test completed, 1 failed ```
